name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-32
title: tdd-step.py uses non-portable Unicode heavy-line escapes via literal hex bytes
description: tdd-step.py _rbt_ok and _rbt_alert (lines 22-29) emit the heavy ASCII line characters using f-string interpolation of literal byte escapes (xe2 x94 x81) inside a str. On Python 3 these get encoded as those literal characters, but the source intent is clearly the heavy-rule unicode glyph U+2501. Other rabbit scripts use the actual character. Use the unicode literal directly for clarity and to avoid encoding surprises on non-UTF-8 stdout.
